page: Amazon Web Services g7e.48xlarge is a graphics-intensive, memory-enhanced server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8559C processors operating at 2.4 GHz, with 96 physical cores and 320 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 2048.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 10.67 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, it bundles eight NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPUs with a combined 768 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of 15200 GB of NVMe SSD, and network connectivity delivers a baseline bandwidth of 400 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier (top 10%) across multiple compute, memory, and storage dimensions. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores are in the top decile, aided by advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). Memory bandwidth tests, both cached small-block and uncached large-block, also rank in the top 10%. The instance demonstrates exceptional capabilities in LLM inference, achieving top-tier speeds for prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models, alongside top-tier performance in ray tracing, image processing, and database operations.

Given its dense hardware profile, the g7e.48xlarge offers high resource density, combining massive GPU acceleration, extensive DDR5 memory, and high-capacity local NVMe storage on a single instance. This configuration minimizes the need for multi-node clustering for large workloads, optimizing qualitative cost efficiency for resource-heavy deployments. It is highly suited for large-scale LLM inference, deep learning training, high-fidelity ray tracing, complex physical simulations, and massive database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services g7e.48xlarge is a high-density, graphics-intensive server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated vCPUs via Intel Xeon 8559C processors, 2048.0 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory, and eight bundled NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPUs with 768 GB of total VRAM. Storage is supported by 15200 GB of local NVMe SSD, complemented by a 400 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data ranks this instance in the top tier (top 10%) for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, database operations, and LLM inference across small, medium, and large models. Its hardware profile makes it highly efficient for workloads requiring simultaneous massive compute, GPU acceleration, and high-speed local storage without the overhead of multi-node clustering. It is optimized for large-scale LLM inference, ray tracing, data-intensive simulations, and high-throughput database applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.48xlarge features 192 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 2048 GB DDR5 RAM, 8 NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPUs, and 15200 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.48xlarge is a Nitro-based instance with 192 vCPUs, 2048 GB RAM, 8 NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs, and 15200 GB NVMe SSD.
tagline: A high-density accelerated instance combining eight Blackwell GPUs, two terabytes of DDR5 memory, and high-speed local NVMe storage.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPUs with 768 GB total VRAM
192 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 2048 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory
15200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 400 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier benchmark performance in LLM inference, ray tracing, and database operations
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector mathematics
